steps.title: Identify the structure on each side
steps.content: The sentence reads: "For Aldo Leopold, the CCC was implicated in what ______." The word what introduces a noun clause functioning as the object of in. This is not a standalone question — it is embedded inside a declarative statement.

steps.title: Apply the rule: embedded clauses use statement word order and no question mark
steps.content:
Embedded noun clauses follow subject-verb order, not verb-subject (inverted) order, and the sentence ends with a period.
  • Choice A — he viewed as unnecessary development.: "...in what he viewed as unnecessary development." — subject he before verb viewed; period ends the statement. Correct embedded-clause form. ✓
  • Choice B — he viewed as unnecessary development?: "...in what he viewed as unnecessary development?" — word order is correct but the question mark is wrong for an embedded clause inside a declarative sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — did he view as unnecessary development?: "...in what did he view as unnecessary development?" — inverted question word order and a question mark; both are wrong for an embedded clause. ✗
  • Choice D — did he view as unnecessary development.: "...in what did he view as unnecessary development." — inverted word order is wrong for an embedded clause, even though the period is correct. ✗
That is the correct answer.
